items.title: HHS/FDA requests sweeping testosterone-therapy label changes, easing age-related use
items.summary: HHS, via the FDA, announced it is requesting updates to prescribing information for all testosterone replacement therapy products after a review of new evidence. The requested changes would remove the limitation of use for age-related hypogonadism, narrow the prostate-cancer contraindication to metastatic disease only, and reflect the TRAVERSE trial's finding of no meaningful increase in major cardiovascular events. The revisions are a request to manufacturers, to be implemented through the standard labeling process.
items.why: Directly bears on Protocol's hormone-therapy line — a more permissive regulatory stance on TRT for age-related low testosterone could expand the eligible patient pool and de-stigmatise prescribing, while raising the bar on patient education and monitoring.

items.title: Cizzle Bio completes US lab validation of CIZ1B early lung-cancer blood test
items.summary: Cizzle Bio announced laboratory validation of its CIZ1B biomarker blood test at OmniHealth Diagnostics, a CLIA-certified lab in Dallas, clearing a key step toward US clinical availability. CIZ1B is an ELISA-based immunoassay that detects a protein variant linked to early-stage lung cancer using less than a tablespoon of blood. The company said it is now finalising launch-readiness activities for near-term availability to US providers.
items.why: Adds a low-cost, blood-based single-cancer early-detection assay to a fast-growing menu — relevant context for how Protocol positions SPOT-MAS multi-cancer detection against cheaper, organ-specific blood tests.
